Oracc: CAMS/GKAB — CAMS Geography of Knowledge Corpus

TCL 06, 11 [procedures for intercalation][via cams/gkab]

Top
t.e. 1t.e. 1

ina a-mat d60 u an-tu₄ liš-lim

(t.e. 1) By the command of Anu and Antu, may it go well.

Obverse
o 1o 1

mul₂U₄.KA.DU₈.A a-dir 30 ina EN.NUN.USAN AN.GE₆ GAR-ma : mul₂SIM.MAḪ a-dir 30 in EN.NUN.MURU₂.BA AN.GE₆ GAR-ma

(o 1) The Leopard is dark (means) the Moon will undergo an eclipse in the evening watch. The Swallow is dark (means) the Moon will undergo an eclipse in the middle watch.

o 22

mul₂GU.LA a-dir 30 ina EN.NUN.U₄.ZAL.LA AN.GE₆ GAR-ma : lu-ma-šu₂ gab-bi ŠU.BI.AŠ.AM₃ mul₂ALLA ḫi-pi₂

(o 2) Gula is dark (means) the Moon will undergo an eclipse in the dawn watch. All constellations, ditto. The Crab (gloss: break)

o 33

dUDU.IDIM LU ḫi-pi₂ḫi-pi₂MEŠ ḫi-pi₂TEu₂ 15 MUL-MEŠ ša₂-niš dbe-let—DINGIR-MEŠ ana dŠUL.PA.E₃.A iṭ-ḫe

(o 3) planet ... (gloss: break) (gloss: break) ... (gloss: break) they approach. Ištar of the stars; alternatively, Belet-ili, approaches Šulpae.

o 44

EN TI-šu₂ gab-bi dIZI.GAR u dṣal-lum-mu-u₂ DU₃uš ša₂-niš 30 ina DU₆ IGI-ma ana mul₂ALLA TE-ma

(o 4) For all of its life it makes meteors and comets. Alternatively, the moon appears in Tašritu (Month VII) and approaches the Crab.

o 55

MUL₂.MUL₂ mul₂ŠUDUN KURud MUL₂.MUL₂ ša₂ U₄ 1-KAM mul₂ŠUDUN mul₂GU₄.AN ša₂ U₄ 1-KAM IGI.LA₂ EN U₄ 20-KAM NU IGI.LA₂

(o 5) The Bristle reaches the Yoke. The Bristle of day 1 (is) the Yoke. The Bull of Heaven that appeared on day 1 does not appear (again) until day 20.

o 66

ša₂ GU₄ MUL₂.MUL₂ ša₂ SIG mul₂UG₅.GA-MEŠ ša₂ ŠU šu-ku-du ša₂ NE mul₂MAR.GID₂.DA ša₂ KIN mul₂a-ru₆ ša₂ DU₆ mul₂UR.GI₇

(o 6) Of Ayyaru (Month II), the Bristle. Of Simanu (Month III), the Raven. Of Duʾuzu (Month IV), the Arrow. Of Anu (Month V), the Wagon. Of Ululu (Month VI), the Eagle. Of Tašritu (Month VII), the Dog.

o 77

ša₂ APIN mul₂GIR₂.TAB ša₂ GAN mul₂MA₂.GUR₈ ša₂ AB AN ša₂ ZIZ₂ ḫar-ri-ri ša₂ ŠE 1(IKU)gana₂ an-nu-u₂ MUL₂.MUL₂ ša₂ ITI-MEŠ gab-bi IZI.ŠUB

(o 7) Of Arahsamnu (Month VIII), the Scorpion. Of Kislimu (Month IX), the Barge. Of Ṭebetu (Month X), Mars. Of Šabaṭu (Month XI), the Vole. Of Addaru (Month XII), the Field. This (with) the Bristle, of all months (means) stroke of lightning.

o 88

MEa GARan šum₄-ma MUL₂.MUL₂ e-la-nu dUDU.IDIM IZI.ŠUB RA šum₄-ma KI.TA i-ṣa

(o 8) You make a prediction. If the Bristle is above the planet: the stroke of lightning will devastate. If it is below (the planet): it will be harmless.

o 99

ša₂-niš 1(IKU)gana₂ a-dir 1(IKU)gana₂ ina itiBARA₂ IGI.LA₂ en-na EN GU₄ NU IGI.LA₂ lib₃-bu-u₂ AN.GE₆-šu₂ u lu-ma-šu₂ gab-bi KI.MIN-ma ina ITI DIRI AN.GE₆ ana UGU iq-ta-bi

(o 9) Alternatively, the Field is dark. The Field appears in Nisannu (Month I). Now, until Ayyaru (Month II) it does not appear, meaning its eclipse and the constellations ditto (do not appear either). In an intercalary month, he has predicted an eclipse concerning it.

o 1010

PA GAN ḪANIŠ ŠULLAT₂ NU im-ḫaṣ pi-ir-du ša₂ AN.GE₆ šu₂-u ina UGU KUR ŠUBdi u KUR TUŠab ina BARA₂ AN.GE₆ GAR ina 18 MU-MEŠ ana GU₄ i-tar

(o 10) .... It does not beat Haniš (and) Šullat. It is the pirdu of the eclipse. On account of that, a land will be abandoned and a land will be settled. In Nisannu it undergoes an eclipse. In 18 years, it returns to Ayyaru.

o 1111

ina 1.36 MU-MEŠ BAR BAR-ma LU₂ šu šip-ri DU₃uš dib-bi ul i-qat-tu-u₂ 12 ša₂ mul₂BABBAR ana UGU DAḪ-ma ina 1.48

(o 11) In 96 years, Nisannu is (again) Nisannu. That man does work (but) matters are not finished. You add on top the 12 (years) of the White Star (Jupiter), and in 1 48 years it is all finished. He does great work.

o 1212

gab-bi i-qat-tu-u₂ šip-ri GALu₂ DU₃uš

o 1313

pi-ir-du ša₂ AN.GE₆ dAMAR.UTU ne₂-pe-šu ki-i DU₃uš lu₂KUR₂ is-si-kip ŠU₂ dUTU gab-bi ana UGU qa-bi

(o 13) The pirdu of an eclipse. When Marduk performed the procedure, the enemy was overthrown. It is said concerning all settings of the Sun.

o 1414

aš₂-ša₂ U₄ 1-KAM ša₂ itiBARA₂ dSAG₃.ME.GAR KI 30 ŠU₂u₂ U₄ 12 AN.GE₆ 30 GARnu 3.20 ša₂ ina EŠ₂.GAR₃ iq-bu-u₂ ana U₄ 20-KAM 3.20 UŠ₂

(o 14) If on day 1 of Nisannu (Month I) Jupiter set with the Moon, (and) on day 12 an eclipse of the Moon took place: the king -- as they say in the Series (Enūma Anu Ellil) -- the king will die on day 20.

o 1515

U₄ 20-KAM U₄ 12 ana 12 U₄-MEŠ EGIR AN.GE₆ 3.20 UŠ₂ ša₂ NE u GAN KI.MIN-ma U₄ 1-KAM U₄ 13 šaṭ-ri aš₂-šu₂ U₄ 2 ša₂ GU₄

(o 15) Day 20 (is) day 12. In 12 days after the eclipse, the king will die. Of Abu (Month V)and Kislimu (Month IX), ditto. Day 1 (and) day 13 are written because of day 2 of Ayyaru (Month II).

o 1616

ana ddele-bat KI.MIN ša₂ dGU₄.U₄ dSAG.UŠ u dṣal-bat-a-nu KI.MIN-ma

(o 16) For Venus, ditto. Of Mercury, Saturn, and Mars, ditto.

o 1717

ina DU₆ U₄ 14 AN.GE₆ EN.NUN.USAN ina mul₂LU 30 GAR mul₂BABBAR u GENNA ina mul₂LU mul₂UR.A mul₂PA u mul₂RIN₂

(o 17) (If) in Tašritu (Month VII) on day 14, the Moon undergoes an eclipse in the Hireling during the evening watch (and) the White Star (Jupiter) and Saturn are in the Hireling, the Lion, Pabilsag, or the Scales -- the opposite of the Hireling -- you tighten a straight string. It stands there and .... You observe Mercury's place in the zodiacal signs.

o 1818

me₂-ḫer ša₂ mul₂LU GU SI.SA₂ DUB₂-šu₂ GUB-ma SIG SIG ŠE.BAR.RA ŠE.GAR.RA KI GU₄.U₄ in KI-MEŠ URU₃-ma

o 1919

GUB-ma SIG-ma mul₂GU₄.U₄ <<GUB>> AN SIG ki GU₄.U₄ KUR₄-ma AN MAḪ ddele-bat ina ŠA₃ SIG ILLU DU <<ILLU>> dele-bat ina ŠA₃ KUR₄

(o 19) (If) Mercury stands (there) and is faint:1 light rain. When Mercury is bright, heavy rain. (If) Venus is faint inside it: a flood will flow. (If) Venus is bright inside it: an enemy attack will become weak; abundance. In the the evening watch, the middle watch, the dawn watch, for all the constellations it is the same.

o 2020

ZI KUR₂ SIG-ma nu-ḫu-uš ina EN.NUN.USAN EN.NUN.MURU₂.BA EN.NUN.U₄.ZAL.LA ša₂ lu-maš gab-bi 1-ma

o 2121

U₄ d30 AN.GE₆ GAR ziq-pi a-mur U₄ ša₂ 20 ana ŠA₃ KUR pi-šir E

(o 21) When the Moon undergoes an eclipse, observe the culminating stars. On the day of the Sun predict an interpretation concerning the land.

o 2222

AN.GE₆ ša₂ 2? IGI-ma GUu₂ ana DU₃-ka šal-ša₂ GE₆ šal-ša₂ ME!(DIŠ) ITI AN.GE₆-ka u₃ ina 18-ka 1.30 ME NIMa šal-šu₂

(o 22) (If) a 2nd eclipse appears, then to make your string, one-third of the night-time (is) one-third of the daytime. In the month of your eclipse and in your 18(th preceding year),2 (the eclipse took place) 1;30° after sunrise. A third of the day of your 18(th preceding year) is 1°. You add 1° to 1;30° and (it is) 2;30°. Add 1°, the period of visibility, on top and (it is) 3;30°. You subtract from it 3, the (length of) daytime of Nisannu, and 0;30° is remaining. You say (this is) after sunset.

o 2323

ša₂ u₄-mu ša₂ 18-ka 1 1 ana 1.30 DAḪ-ma 2.30 1 ta-mar-tu₂ ana UGU DU-ma 3.30 3 u₄-me ša₂ BAR!(PA)

o 2424

TA ŠA₃ E₁₁-ma re-ḫi 30 GE₆ DU E1

o 2525

ki-i ina 18-ka ina BAR ITI AN.GE₆-ka 1.30 GE₆ DU šal-šu₂ ša₃ GE₆ ša₂ BAR 1 3 GE₆ ša₂ BARA₂ TA ŠA₃ E₁₁-ma ša₂ re-ḫi 1.30 ME NIM E

(o 25) If in your 18(th preceding year), in Nisannu, the month of your eclipse, the after sunset was 1;30°: one-third of the night-time of Nisannu is 1°. You subtract (the 1;30°) from 3°, the night-time of Nisannu, and you say of the remaining 1;30°, "after sunrise".

o 2626

ki-i ina 18-ka ina BAR 30 ana ŠU₂ 20 šal-šu₂ ša₂ 3 ME ša₂ BAR 1 1 BADtu₂ ana UGU DU-ma 2 : 30 TA 2 E₁₁-ma re-ḫi 1.30 GE₆ DU E

(o 26) If in your 18(th preceding year), in Nisannu, 0;30° was for the setting of the sun: one-third of 3°, the daytime of Nisannu, is 1°. You add 1°, the period of visibility, on top and (it is) 2°. : You subtract 0;30° from 2° and 1;30° is remaining. You say (it is) "after sunset".

o 2727

ki-i ina 18-ka ina BAR 30 GE₆ ana ZALAG₂ šal-šu₂ ša₂ 3 GE₆ ša₂ BAR 1 <<1>> 1 BADtu₂ ana UGU DU-ma 2 : 30 TA 2 E₁₁-ma re-ḫi 1.30 ME NIMa DU₁₁.GA

(o 27) If in your 18(th preceding year), in Nisannu, it was 30° of night-time until day break: one-third of 3°, the night-time of Nisannu, is 1°. You add 1°, the period of visibility, on top and (it is) 2°. : You subtract 0;30° from 2° and 1;30° is remaining. You say (it is) "after sunrise".

o 2828

šal-šu₂ 2ta ŠU-MIN-ME : TILti₃ TILti₃ um-ma 2ta ŠU-MIN-MEŠ šal-šu₂ U₄ SILIM U₄ SILIM um-ma2

(o 28) One-third, two-thirds : totality, totality they say. Two-thirds, one third: favourable day, favourable day, they say.

o 2929

LA₂u₂ u kun-nu ana IGI-ka šum₄-ma ina 18 BAR 1 ṭi-pi KI-šu₂ ul ṭe-pi GU₄ ša₂ EGIR-šu₂ kun-nu šal-šu₂

(o 29) For you to foresee a hollow or full (month). If in the 18(th preceding year) Nisannu (began on day) 1 (and) an addition was not added to it: Ayyaru, which is after it, is full. One-third of moonset-to-sunrise and sunrise-to-moonset is 6. You subtract that from sunrise-to-moonset of day 1 of Ayyaru, and (if) it is less than (in) Nisannu that was before it, Ayyaru of your new year will be full.

o 3030

ša₂ ŠU₂ u NA 6 šu₂ TA NA ša₂ U₄ 1-KAM ša₂ GU₄ ZI-ma al-la BAR ša₂ UGU-šu₂ LA₂ GU₄ ša₂ MU-ka eš-še-tu₂ kun-nu3

o 3131

ma-la ina 18-ka kun-nu u ṭi-pi KI-šu₂ la ṭe-pu-u₂ u₃ nis-ḫu TA ŠA₃ ZI-ma al-la ITI

(o 31) As many (months) as were full in your 18(th preceding year) and to which an addition had not been added and a subtraction subtracted from it, and which were less than the month before it: you predict (them) as full (in the coming year).

o 3232

ša₂ UGU-šu₂ LA₂u₂ ana kun-nu E ki-i ina 18-ka kun-nu ṭi-pi KI-šu₂ la ṭe-pu-u₂ u₃ nis-ḫu TA

(o 32) If in your 18(th preceding year) it (the month) was full, an addition had not been added to it and a subtraction was subtracted from it and it was less than3 10 degrees: you predict (it) as full (in the coming year).

o 3333

lib₃-bi ZI-ma al-la 10 UŠ LA₂u₂ ana kun-nu E šum₄-ma ina 18-ka kun-nu u ṭi-pi KI-šu₂ ṭe-pi ana GURru E

(o 33) If in your 18(th preceding year) it (the month) was full and an addition had been added to it: you predict (it) as hollow.

o 3434

aš₂-šu₂ 30 ana 20 NIM GUR U₄-MEŠ U₄ 15-MEŠ aš₂-šu₂ 30 ana 20 SIG GIN U₄-MEŠ U₄ 13-MEŠ aš₂-šu₂ 30 NIM DAB U₄ 15-MEŠ U₄ 16-MEŠ aš₂-šu₂ <SIG?> DAB

(o 34) If the Moon is high to the Sun: hollow. The days (of first moonset after sunrise) are 15th days. If the Moon is low to the Sun: full. The days are 13th days. If the Moon reaches maximum latitude: the days are 15th days (and) 16th days. If it reaches minimum latitude, the days are 12th days (and) 13th days. Nisannu .....

o 3535

U₄ 12-MEŠ U₄ 13-MEŠ BAR SIG ZI-MEŠ aš₂-šu₂ 30 KASKAL-MIN ša₂ NIM DAB 3 GUR-AM₃!(AN) aš₂-šu₂ KASKAL-MIN ša₂ SIG DAB 3 GIN-MEŠ TA BAR U₄ 1-MEŠ NIM U₄ 14-MEŠ SIG TA DU₆ U₄ 1-MEŠ SIG U₄ 14-MEŠ NIM

(o 35) If the Moon takes a path of maximum latitude: 3 (months) will be hollow. If it takes a path of minimum latitude: 3 (months) will be full. From Nisannu the 1st days (of the month) will be high, the 14th days low. From Tašritu (Month VII) the 1st days will be low, the 14th days high.

o 3636

GABA.RI 36!(34) ana DU₃-ka TA BAR ša₂ 36 6 ITI BAR GUR-ma 40 ša₂ ŠU₂ u NA ša₂ DU₆ GIŠ-ma TA NA ša₂ U₄ 1

(o 36) For you to work out the equivalent of 36 (years). From Nisannu (Month I) of the 36(th year) you go back 6 months, Nisannu, and you write 0;40 of the moonset-to-sunrise and sunrise-to-moonset of Tašritu, and you subtract (it) from the sunrise-to-moonset of day 1 of Nisannu of the 36(th year); and if it extends beyond 10°, you add the entire moonset-to-sunrise and sunrise-to-moonset on top.

o 3737

ša₂ BAR ša₂ 36 ZI-ma šum₄-ma al-la 10 UŠ LA₂ ŠU₂ u NA bal-ṭu-ut ana UGU DAḪ PAP 40 ša₂ ŠU₂ u NA TA NA ša₂ MURU₂ ITI

(o 37) Otherwise, you subtract 0;40 of the moonset-to-sunrise and sunrise-to-moonset from the sunrise-to-moonset of the middle of the month. You subtract 0;40 of the moonrise-to-sunset and sunset-to-moonrise from the sunset-to-moonrise.

o 3838

ZIaḫ 40 ša₂ ME u GE₆ TA GE₆ ZIaḫ

Reverse
r 1r 1

6 ITI GUR-ma NA ša₂ IGI 20 ša₂ DU₆ u APIN tam-mar šum₄-ma 6 NA ša₂ APIN u₃ 3 NA ša₂ DU₆ 3 NA ša₂ DU₆ ša₂ al-la

(r 1) You go back 6 months and you examine the sunrise-to-moonset of before the Sun for Tašritu (Month VII) and Arahsamnu (Month VIII). If the sunrise-to-moonset of Arahsamnu is 6 and the sunrise-to-moonset of Tašritu is 3, you subtract 3, by which the sunrise-to-moonset of Tašritu is smaller than that of Arahsamnu, from the sunrise-to-moonset of day 1 of Nisannu (Month I) of your new year; and (if) it is greater than 12°, you predict Ayyaru (Month II), which is after it, as hollow.

r 22

ša₂ APIN ma-ṭu-u₂ TA NA ša₂ U₄ 1 ša₂ BAR ša₂ MU-ka eš-še-tu₂ ZI-ma al-la 12 UŠ DIRI GU₄ ša₂ EGIR-šu₂ GUR E

r 33

šum₄-ma 3 NA ša₂ APIN u₃ 6 NA ša₂ DU₆ 3 NA DU₆ ša₂ al-la ša₂ APIN DIRI KI NA ša₂ U₄ 1 ša₂ BAR

(r 3) If the sunrise-to-moonset of Arahsamnu is 3 and the sunrise-to-moonset of Tašritu is 6, you add 3, by which the sunrise-to-moonset of Tašritu is greater than that of Arahsamnu, to the sunrise-to-moonset of day 1 of Nisannu of your new year; and if it is greater than that of Nisannu of your new year, full; if less, hollow.

r 44

ša₂ MU-ka eš-še-tu₂ TAB-ma šum₄-ma al-la NA ša₂ BAR ša₂ MU-ka eš-še-tu₂ DIRI kun-nu šum₄-ma LA₂ GUR

r 55

TA BAR ša₂ MU-ka eš-še-tu₂ 18 GUR-ma KI.LA₂ u₄-mu u GE₆ ša₂ BAR ša₂ 18-ka tam-mar šum₄-ma BAR ša₂ 18-ka U₄ 15

(r 5) From Nisannu (Month I) of your new year you go back 18 (years) and you examine the weight4 of the daytime and night-time of Nisannu of your 18(th previous year).

(r 5) If in Nisannu of your 18(th year) on day 15 the daytime is 6 double-hours and the night-time is 6 double-hours (and) from (day) 15 to 20 of Nisannu the daytime is 6 double-hours, 10°, the night-time is 5 5/6 double-hours: you subtract 5° 4 nindanu from the sunrise-to-moonset of day 1 of Ayyaru of your 18(th year); and if it is greater than 12°, ḫollow; if less, Ayyaru of your new year is full.

r 66

6 DANNA u₄-mu u₃ 6 DANNA GE₆ TA 15 EN 20 ša₂ BAR 6 DANNA 10 UŠ u₄-mu 5.5/6 DANNA GE₆ 5 UŠ 40 NINDA

r 77

TA NA ša₂ U₄ 1-KAM ša₂ GU₄ ša₂ 18-ka ZI-ma šum₄-ma al-la 12 UŠ DIRI GUR šum₄-ma LA₂ GU₄ ša₂ MU-ka eš-še-tu₂ GIN

r 88

GABA.RI U₄.NA₂.A ana DU₃-ka šum₄-ma BAR ša₂ MU-ka eš-še-tu₂ 27 25 KUR 3.20 ME 2.40 GE₆ 3.20 A.RA₂ 4

(r 8) For you to work out the equivalent of the day of the moon's disappearance. If in Nisannu (Month I) of your new year, on (day) 27, the moonrise-to-sunrise is 25, the daytime is 3;20, the night-time is 2;40: 3;20 times 4 is 13;20. You subtract 13;20 from 25 and (the moon) will be late (by) 11;40. On (day) 28, 11;40 is remaining for the Sun.

r 99

13.20 13.20 TA 25 ZI-ma 11.40 uḫ-ḫur U₄ 28 11.40 ana UGU 20 re-ḫi 13.20 TA 11.40 ZI-ma

(r 9) You subtract 13;20 from 11;40 and on day 29 the Moon will have passed the Sun by 1;40. You add 13;20 on top of 1;40 and (it is) 15. On day 30, the Moon will have passed the Sun by 15.

r 1010

U₄ 29 1.40 30 ana 20 DIBiq 13.20 ana muḫ-ḫi 1.40 TAB-ma 15 U₄ 30 <<U₄>> 15 30 ana 20 DIBiq šum₄-ma 27 15 KUR

(r 10) If on (day) 27 the moonrise-to-sunrise is 15, 3;20 times 4 is 13;20. You subtract 13;20 from 15 and (the Moon) will be late (by) 1;40. On (day) 28 1;40 is remaining for the Sun. You subtract 1;40 from 13;20 and on day 29 (the Moon) will have passed the Sun by 11;40.

r 1111

3.20 A.RA₂ 4 13.20 13.20 TA 15 ZI-ma 1.40 uḫ-ḫur 28 1.40 ana 20 re-ḫi 1.40 TA 13.20

r 1212

ZI-ma 11.40 U₄ 29 ana 20 DIBiq šum₄-ma 27 24 KUR 4 ME 2 GE₆ 4 A.RA₂ 4 16 16 TA 24 ZI-ma

(r 12) If on (day) 27 the moonrise-to-sunrise is 24, the daytime is 4, the night-time 2: 4 times 4 is 16. You subtract 16 from 24 and (the Moon) will be late (by) 8. On (day) 28, 8 will be remaining for the Sun. You subtract 16 from 8 and on (day) 29 (the Moon) will have passed the Sun by 8.

r 1313

8 uḫ-ḫur 28 8 ana UGU 20 re-ḫi 16 TA 8 ZI-ma 29 8 ana 20 DIBiq miš₂-lu ša₂ 16 8

(r 13) Half of 16 is 8. You add on top of 8 and (it is) 16. On (day) 29, at the setting of the Sun, the sunrise-to-moonset is 16. If the daytime is longer than the night-time, you multiply the daytime by 4. If the night-time is longer than the daytime, you multiply the night-time by 4. You examine the weight of daytime and night-time and you take the path with the excess.

r 1414

ana UGU 8 TAB-ma 16 29 ina ŠU₂ 20 16 NA šum₄-ma ME ana GE₆ DIRI u₄-mu A.RA₂ 4 tal-lak šum₄-ma GE₆ ana u₄-mu

r 1515

DIRI GE₆ A.RA₂ 4 tal-lak KI.LA₂ ME u GE₆ IGI-ma KI DIRI KASKAL tal-lak

r 1616

GABA.RI ITI ana IGI-ka itiŠU U₄ 1-KAM 20 NA 7 KI 20 TAB-ma 27 20 A.RA₂ 27 9 9 KI 20 TAB-ma 29

(r 16) For you to foresee the equivalent of the month. In Duʾuzu (Month IV) on day 1 the sunrise-to-moonset is 20. You add 7 to 20 and (it is) 27. 20 times 27 is 9. You add 9 to 20 and it is 29.

r 1717

1/2-šu₂ GIŠ-ma 14.30 NE GUR 14.30 NA ki-i LA₂u₂ GUR ki-i DIRI GIN TA UGU 25 ana a-ḫa-ti

(r 17) You write its 1/2 and (it is) 14;30. Abu (Month V) is hollow. If 14;30 is smaller than the sunrise-to-moonset, hollow; if greater, full. (Anything) above 25 you put to the sides. If it is less than 25 you add all. For all months, ditto.

r 1818

ŠUBdi ki-i al-la 25 i-ṣa gab-bi ta-ṭep-pe ša₂ ITI-MEŠ gab-bi ŠU.BI.AŠ.AM₃

r 1919

ša₂!(4-)niš NA ša₂ U₄ 1 A.RA₂ 20 DU-ma KI.MIN-ma ša₂!(4-)niš ki kun-nu 6 ki GUR 12 ta-ṭep-pe

(r 19) Alternatively, you multiply the sunrise-to-moonset of day 1 by 20 and ditto. Alternatively, if full you add 6; if hollow, 12.

r 2020

12 NA ana IGI-ka U₄.NA₂.A ša₂ ŠE ša₂ UGU BAR ša₂ MU-ka TILtu₄ IGI šum₄-ma al-la U₄.NA₂.A ša₂ ŠE ša₂ UGU BAR

(r 20) For you to foresee 12, the sunrise-to-moonset. You observe the day of the moon's disappearance of Addaru (Month XII) that was before the Nisannu (Month I) of your (just) completed year.

(r 20) If it is greater than the day of the moon's disappearance of Addaru that was before the Nisannu of your new year, you subtract whatever is greater from the sunrise-to-moonset of the Nisannu of your new year; and you predict that Nisannu will be full.

r 2121

ša₂ MU-ka eš-še-tu₂ DIRI mim-ma ša₂ DIRI TA NA ša₂ BAR ša₂ MU-ka eš-še-tu₂ ZI-ma BAR GIN E šum₄-ma LA₂ mim-ma

(r 21) If it is smaller, you add whatever is smaller to the sunrise-to-moonset of the Nisannu of your new year; and you predict that Nisannu will be hollow.

r 2222

ša₂ LA₂u₂ KI NA ša₂ BAR ša₂ MU-ka eš-še-tu₂ TAB-ma BAR 30 E

r 2323

ŠEG₃ u ILLU ana DU₃-ka!(KI) 1.12 ša₂ SAG₃.ME.GAR 1 ŠU 4 ša₂-niš 16 ša₂ dele-bat 46 ša₂-niš 13 ša₂ GU₄.U₄ 59 ša₂ GENNA 1.19 ša₂-niš 47 ša₂ AN

(r 23) For you to work out rain and flood. 1 12 is for Jupiter. 1 sixty, 4; alternatively, 16, is for Venus. 46; alternatively 13, is for Mercury. 59 is for Saturn. 1 19; alternatively, 47, is for Mars.

r 2424

ŠEG₃ u ILLU ana DU₃-ka ḫaš-šu₂ ša₂ 9 UŠ 9(ŠAR₂) ana EGIR-ka GUR-ma 10.30 ša₂ ḫi-pi₂

(r 24) For you to work out rain and flood. You turn back behind you one-fifth of 9 sixties, 9 × 3600, and 10 30 of (gloss: break) of Saturn you turn back.5

r 2525

ša₂ GENNA GUR 40 MU-MEŠ ša₂ dele-bat 30 ša₂ GU₄.U₄ ana EGIR-ki GAR-ma ILLU ka-<a>-ma-<<a>>-nu ša₂-niš ŠEG₃ ana DU₃ 30 MU-MEŠ

(r 25) You put behind you 40 years of Venus, 30 of Mercury, and (there will be) regular flooding. Alternatively, to work out rain. You add 30 years of Saturn, 41 years of Jupiter, ... 1 23 ... (gloss: break). There and then, in these months, rain will fall from the skies.

r 2626

ša₂ GENNA 41 MU-MEŠ ša₂ SAG₃.ME.GAR DI 1.23 ta-ṭep-pa₂ il-la ḫi-pi₂KI!(ITI)U₄.BA ina ITI-MEŠ an-nu-tu

r 2727

ŠEG₃ ina šam-me il-la-ku-nu ina 1.12 MU-MEŠ dṣal-lum-mu-u₂ ša₂ ina KUN-ME IGI ina KUN-ma IGI

(r 27) In 1 12 years a comet which appeared in the Tails will appear (again) in the Tails. In 36 years it will answer.

r 2828

ina 36 MU-MEŠ ip-pal ina 21 MU-MEŠ ŠEG₃ ana ŠEG₃ ILLU ana ILLU ip-pal ina 21 MU-ME ri-i-bi ana ri-i-bi ip-pal ina 6 me 54 MU-MEŠ ḫi-pi₂

(r 28) In 21 years rain will answer rain, flood (will answer) flood. In 21 years earthquake will answer earthquake. In 6 hundred 54 years (gloss: break).

r 2929

mul₂UR.A mul₂ABSIN mul₂GIR₂.TAB PA.BIL MAŠ₂ u KUN-ME PAP 6 KI-MEŠ ša₂ 30 ina ŠA₃-ši-na TUR₃ NIGIN₂-ma ana 8i u₄-mu TU₁₅ DU₁₀tu-u ZIa

(r 29) The Lion the Furrow, the Scorpion, Pabilsag, the Goatfish and the Tails: total 6 zodiacal signs inside which (if) the Moon is surrounded by a lunar halo on the eighth day a good wind will arise.

r 3030

ILLU ina ITI-ka KI dUDU.IDIM-MEŠ ta-maš-šaḫ šal-ša₂-šu₂ DIRI BAR ba-ri-ṣa ša₂ ŠU ba-<ri>-ṣa ša₂ AB 1 KUŠ₃

(r 30) You measure the flood in your month with the planets. Īts third .... You put the barīṣu of Duʾuzu (Month IV), the barīṣu of Ṭebetu (Month X), 1 cubit of shadow, a third of the dappu, 19, appearance and disappearance. 2 hundred 30 months completes 19 years.

r 3131

GISSU šal-šu₂ ša₂ da-ap-pi 19 IGI u ŠU₂ GARan 2 me 30 ITI 19!(18) MU-MEŠ GI MU 36 SIG 30 15 NA ma-diš SIG

(r 31) Year 36, Simanu (Month III) (began on day) 30 (of the previous month). Sunset-to-moonset was 15°. (The Moon) was very low.

r 3232

itiDU₆ 5 9 1.30 BAR 21 12 u 20 DU₆ 4 4 GIR₂ GIR₂ : APIN 10 20 GU₄ KIN u AB 21 12 u 20 NIM

(r 32) Tašritu (Month VII): 5, 9, 1;30. Nisannu (Month I): 21, 12, and 20. Tašritu (Month VII): 4, 4 ... : Arahsamnu (Month VIII): 10, 20. Ayyaru (Month II), Ululu (Month VI), and Ṭebetu (Month X): 21 12, and 20 ....

r 3333

BARA₂ ša₂ ana UB UB TI SIG 21 12 u 20

(r 33) Nisannu that to .... Simanu (Month III): 21, 12, and 20.

r 3434

ina 1.05 MU-MEŠ MI 30 GI? : 50 1 PAP 40 1 ZUM NE ŠEG₃ u ILLU ša₂ GU₄.U₄ 2 A.RA₂ 5 U₄ LA₂ ša₂ dele-bat 16 3 3 U₄ LA₂

(r 34) In 1 05 years .... : .... Rain and flood of Mercury, 2 times 5 days smaller; of Venus 16 3 3 days smaller.

r 3535

1.30 A.RA₂ 12 18 18 A.RA₂ 5 1.30 1 ŠAR₂ 50 ŠAR₂ 8 ŠAR₂ 4(GEŠʾU) 5 me 1.33 MU-MEŠ 3 ITI MU 5 ITI 10 U₄.DA.ZAL

(r 35) 1;30 times 12 is 18. 18 times 5 is 1 30. 60 three-thousand-six-hundreds, 50 three-thousand-six-hundreds, 8 three-thousand-six-hundreds, 4 six-hundreds, 5 hundred, 1 33 years, 3 months. The intercalation is 5 years, 10 months.

r 3636

16 me 9 MU-MEŠ NIM 1 lim 8 me MU-MEŠ SAR ana ITI-MEŠ : NIM 5 MU-MEŠ 10 ŠAR₂ 50 MU-MEŠ ana ku-us-su ZAḪ₂

(r 36) 16 hundred 9 years .... 1 thousand 8 hundred years ... for the months : .... 5 years 10 three-thousand-six-hundreds, 50 years until the throne is destroyed.

(catchline)
r 3737

šum₄-ma EŠ.BAR 3.20 ana IGI-ka ša₂ dUDU.IDIM-MEŠ ina lu-maš KIN.KIN-ma

(r 37) "If (you wish) to foresee a decision about the king, you seek (the positions) of the planets in the constellations."

(colophon)
r 3838

IM mNIG₂.SUM.MU—d60 A ša₂ mina—qi₂-bit—d60 A mḫun-zu-u₂ lu₂MAŠ.MAŠ d60 u an-tu₄ UNUGki qat₃ md60—DINiṭ A-šu₂

(r 38) Tablet of Nidintu-Anu, son of Ina-qibit-Anu, descendant of Hunzu, incantation priest of Anu and Antu. Uruk, hand of Anu-uballiṭ, his son.

1The meaning of GE₆ DU is known but not the Akkadian reading of it.

2This line is very badly understood.

3The Akkadian values of ŠU₂ and NA are unknown, but their technical meaning is clear.

1 The word order in this line is rather muddled.

2 This a reference to the so-called Saros cycle , the fact that eclipse patterns repeat over a period of about 18 years and 11 days.

3 "More than " is expected here.

4 That is , the weight of water in a water-clock.

5 The remaining lines of the tablet are exceeding difficult to read and understand.
